title: Anthology Of Interest I

jokes.text: I call it the 'Fing-Longer.' Observe. And here we go. There! Pretty long, eh?
jokes.setup: Professor announces invention to operate equipment from great distances
jokes.punchline: It's just a finger extender with an obvious portmanteau name
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor Farnsworth - brilliant scientist with completely ridiculous priorities. The wordplay is simple but perfect for the character.

jokes.text: It's just not very long.
jokes.setup: Farnsworth boasts about his invention being 'really long'
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits it's not very long at all
jokes.explanation: Nice deflation of his own boasting, very economical delivery

jokes.text: As a robot among humans, I never feel accepted at parties or nude beaches.
jokes.setup: Bender explains feeling like an outsider as a robot
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions nude beaches as a normal social venue
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - treats inappropriate venues as mundane while expressing genuine vulnerability

jokes.text: I'm so lonely since I came to the future. Will you be my friend?
jokes.setup: Giant robot towers over city
jokes.punchline: Just wants friendship, completely innocent despite terrifying appearance
jokes.explanation: Classic monster movie subversion - the 'threat' is just lonely and misunderstood

jokes.text: I'm a big robot! And I want a big cereal.
jokes.setup: Giant robot could demand anything from terrified humans
jokes.punchline: Wants proportionally sized breakfast cereal
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly mundane request from a kaiju-sized being, very quotable and character-specific

jokes.text: Put her there, pal! No, your wallet.
jokes.setup: Friendly gesture of shaking hands
jokes.punchline: Actually wants to rob the person
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - even as a lonely giant, he's still a criminal. Excellent misdirection.

jokes.text: That calms the shakes.
jokes.setup: Character drinks alcohol in crisis situation
jokes.punchline: Admits to having alcohol dependency
jokes.explanation: Standard alcoholic character bit, not particularly original but economically delivered

jokes.text: Well, there goes the neighborhood. And another one. We're jerked!
jokes.setup: Buildings being destroyed by giant monsters
jokes.punchline: Casual reaction escalating to euphemistic exclamation
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay and escalation, functionally amusing but not exceptional

jokes.text: What? Two meals in one week?
jokes.setup: Scientists need a guinea pig for dangerous experiment
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg is excited about being fed twice in a week
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg - so poor and hungry he's excited about being experimented on. Great character work.

jokes.text: Gotcha! A guinea pig tricked me!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg volunteers to be test subject
jokes.punchline: Realizes he was 'tricked' into volunteering for dangerous duty
jokes.explanation: Zoidberg's delayed realization and victim mentality is character-appropriate

jokes.text: Who's intimidating who now, big city? Hello, Mr. Chase Manhattan Bank. Deny my credit card application, will you?
jokes.setup: Zoidberg becomes giant and powerful
jokes.punchline: Uses power for petty personal revenge against bank
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - Zoidberg's first instinct with ultimate power is settling credit disputes

jokes.text: Ah, the Apollo Theater. Boo me off-stage on open-mike night? I'll show you!
jokes.setup: Giant Zoidberg continues rampage
jokes.punchline: Targeting venue that rejected his comedy act
jokes.explanation: Continues the pattern of petty revenge, revealing Zoidberg's failed comedy aspirations

jokes.text: Tell it to the claw! Bite my colossal metal ass!
jokes.setup: Two giant monsters fighting
jokes.punchline: Bender's catchphrase upgraded for his new size
jokes.explanation: Good callback adaptation - familiar phrase modified for the situation

jokes.text: Who wants lobster bisque?
jokes.setup: Giant Zoidberg has been killed/cooked
jokes.punchline: Offering his remains as food
jokes.explanation: Dark but logical conclusion - Zoidberg is a lobster-like creature, so this makes practical sense

jokes.text: Want to make Shrinky Dinks?
jokes.setup: Characters dealing with the aftermath of giant creatures
jokes.punchline: Suggesting making children's toys from the remains
jokes.explanation: Absurd non-sequitur that plays on the shrinking theme. Moderately clever but not deeply funny.

jokes.text: I came here with a simple dream... a dream of killing all humans.
jokes.setup: Dying Bender's emotional final words
jokes.punchline: His dream was genocide
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of deathbed speech trope. Perfect Bender - even his dying words are hilariously evil.

jokes.text: And this is how it must end? Who's the real seven-billion-ton robot monster here? Not I.
jokes.setup: Bender philosophically questions who the real monster is
jokes.punchline: Immediately absolves himself despite admitting to genocide plans
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Bender's complete lack of self-awareness even in death

jokes.text: Good night, sweet prince.
jokes.setup: Solemn moment mourning giant Bender's death
jokes.punchline: Hamlet quote applied to murderous robot
jokes.explanation: Classical reference provides dignity to absurd situation, economical and appropriate

jokes.text: I can be impulsive. It just takes me a while.
jokes.setup: Leela claims she can be impulsive
jokes.punchline: Contradicts herself by saying impulse takes time
jokes.explanation: Perfect logical contradiction that defines Leela's character - overthinking everything including spontaneity

jokes.text: Look what I bought on a wild impulse! New boots! They're like my old ones, but with a crazy green stripe!
jokes.setup: Impulsive Leela shows off spontaneous purchase
jokes.punchline: Boots nearly identical to her current ones with minimal change
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leela - even when trying to be wild, she's incredibly conservative and practical

jokes.text: Not like predictable, dull-as-dishwater Leela.
jokes.setup: Professor explains why Leela should inherit his fortune
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest assessment of her personality
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor tactlessness, sets up the irony of what happens next

jokes.text: Hello? New boots?
jokes.setup: Professor calls her predictable after she just showed new boots
jokes.punchline: Leela's indignant reaction to being called dull
jokes.explanation: Good callback to her 'impulsive' purchase, shows her defensiveness about being boring

jokes.text: You've killed me! You've killed me! What have I done? I just told you! You've killed me!
jokes.setup: Professor appears to be dying
jokes.punchline: Repeats the accusation when Leela asks what she did
jokes.explanation: Functional but not exceptional - the repetition works for the character's panic

jokes.text: All right, you're nonchalant. Don't rub our noses in it.
jokes.setup: Leela tries to act nonchalant about the Professor's death
jokes.punchline: Fry calls her out for overdoing the casual act
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Fry notices when someone's acting fake, which is rare for him

jokes.text: I'll call the cops. After I flush some things.
jokes.setup: Crisis situation calls for contacting authorities
jokes.punchline: Bender needs to dispose of evidence first
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - even in crisis, his criminal priorities come first. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: I happen to have mail-order degrees in Murder-ology and Murder-onomy.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg offers to investigate the death
jokes.punchline: Claims expertise in made-up murder sciences with academic-sounding names
jokes.explanation: Great Zoidberg moment - fake credentials in nonsensical fields, but delivered with total confidence

jokes.text: What? How do you spell that? What are you hacking off? My torso? It is! My precious torso!
jokes.setup: Hermes being questioned/threatened
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's being dismembered mid-conversation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and Hermes' bureaucratic reaction to his own dismemberment

jokes.text: What? How do you spell that? What are you hacking off? My torso?
jokes.setup: Hermes apparently being attacked by something off-screen
jokes.punchline: His bureaucratic concern about spelling even while being dismembered
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes characterization - his bureaucratic nature persisting even during mortal peril. Very character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Ain't nothing wrong with murder... as long as I can wet my beak.
jokes.setup: Leela confesses to murder
jokes.punchline: Scruffy's only concern is getting paid to stay quiet
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy - morally flexible but honest about his motivations

jokes.text: I prefer the word extortion. The 'x' makes it sound cool.
jokes.setup: Discussion of blackmail
jokes.punchline: Bender's aesthetic preference for criminal terminology
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - caring about the coolness factor of his crimes. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Okay, that's it. No more killing. Next time, just have a stick of gum.
jokes.setup: Leela has killed multiple people
jokes.punchline: Treats murder like a bad habit she's trying to quit
jokes.explanation: Good casual treatment of extreme behavior, like quitting smoking

jokes.text: Who are you? Scruffy, the janitor. I've never seen you before. I've never seen you neither.
jokes.setup: Murder investigation gathering all suspects
jokes.punchline: Scruffy appears despite never being seen before
jokes.explanation: Sets up Scruffy's running gag of being mysteriously invisible to other characters

jokes.text: See? What smells like boot feet?
jokes.setup: Leela claims the boot print couldn't be hers because she doesn't wear boots
jokes.punchline: Shows her bare feet which apparently smell like boots
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy callback to boot theme, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: My next clue came at 4:15, when the clock stopped. Two hours later, at 4:15... I discovered the murdered body of Amy's dead, deceased corpse.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg presents detective timeline
jokes.punchline: Time makes no sense and uses redundant death descriptions
jokes.explanation: Zoidberg's incompetence as detective, redundant phrasing is character-appropriate but not elegant

jokes.text: Dear Doctor Jerk-berg, if you're reading this, I'm dead. The murderer was...
jokes.setup: Bender's posthumous letter promising to reveal the killer
jokes.punchline: The obvious insult name for Zoidberg and incomplete revelation
jokes.explanation: Good Bender voice - insulting Zoidberg even from beyond the grave. The interrupted revelation is a classic trope but well-executed.

jokes.text: Are we near an Indian graveyard? No? Then it was you!
jokes.setup: Bender claims to have figured out the deaths
jokes.punchline: Uses process of elimination based on horror movie tropes
jokes.explanation: Nice genre-aware joke that uses horror clichés as elimination method

jokes.text: Here's to another lousy millennium.
jokes.setup: Fry in original timeline before being frozen
jokes.punchline: Same toast that led to his freezing in series pilot
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to the show's origin - economical and emotionally resonant for fans

jokes.text: There's only three monsters, kid. Dracula, Blacula and Son of Kong.
jokes.setup: Fry tells his boss about monsters
jokes.punchline: Dismisses concern with oddly specific monster taxonomy
jokes.explanation: Great mix of real and parody monsters presented as fact. Very quotable absurdist logic.

jokes.text: Is one of you I.C. Wiener? If that's his pizza, then I am.
jokes.setup: Fry delivers pizza to a prank name
jokes.punchline: Nibbler claims the fake identity to get free pizza
jokes.explanation: Solid opportunistic humor from Nibbler taking advantage of the prank call

jokes.text: Stephen Hawking! Didn't you invent gravity? Sure. Why not?
jokes.setup: Fry's ignorant question about Stephen Hawking's achievements
jokes.punchline: Hawking's casual acceptance of credit for fundamental physics
jokes.explanation: Perfect combination of Fry's stupidity and Hawking's deadpan humor. Economical and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Isn't your sole duty to break ties in the Senate?
jokes.setup: Al Gore claims his duty is preventing space-time disruption
jokes.punchline: Fry points out his actual constitutional role
jokes.explanation: Solid political humor pointing out the absurdity of Gore's secret sci-fi job

jokes.text: That, and protect the space-time continuum.
jokes.setup: Gore admits his main duty is breaking Senate ties
jokes.punchline: Casually adds universe-saving as secondary responsibility
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and absurdist take on vice presidential duties

jokes.text: Incoming transmission from MCI. It sounds like low rates. I'm in the tub!
jokes.setup: Uhura receives communications as her Star Trek character
jokes.punchline: It's just a telemarketer, responds like regular person
jokes.explanation: Decent subversion of Star Trek communications trope with mundane reality

jokes.text: Incoming transmission from MCI. It sounds like low rates. I'm in the tub!
jokes.setup: Uhura using her communication skills
jokes.punchline: Receiving telemarketing calls and responding like a regular person
jokes.explanation: Decent subversion - using Star Trek communication powers for mundane telemarketing. The 'I'm in the tub' adds domestic normalcy.

jokes.text: Bishop to knight 4. Not every mission can be solved by chess, Deep Blue.
jokes.setup: Computer suggests chess move in crisis situation
jokes.punchline: Gore points out chess isn't always the solution
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Deep Blue and reasonable leadership from Gore

jokes.text: I call it a 'Hawking Hole.' Hey, I saw it first! Who will The Journal of Quantum Physics believe?
jokes.setup: Fry and Hawking argue over naming rights
jokes.punchline: Hawking pulls academic rank
jokes.explanation: Decent academic rivalry joke, realistic character behavior

jokes.text: As an environmentalist, I'm against that.
jokes.setup: Team explains the universe will be destroyed
jokes.punchline: Gore frames universe destruction as an environmental issue
jokes.explanation: Brilliant understatement treating universal annihilation as merely an environmental concern - perfect Gore characterization

jokes.text: I call it a 'Hawking Chamber.' But I missed and wanged my head.
jokes.setup: Hawking continues stealing naming rights
jokes.punchline: Fry's casual description of nearly fatal accident
jokes.explanation: Continues the naming gag with Fry's typical casual attitude toward danger

jokes.text: Something's wrong! Murder isn't working.
jokes.setup: The team tries to kill Fry to prevent universe destruction
jokes.punchline: Gore's frustrated realization that their murder attempt is failing
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy from treating murder as a technical problem to solve

jokes.text: Put the dice away.
jokes.setup: Gary Gygax presumably pulls out D&D dice during crisis
jokes.punchline: Gore has to manage his team's inappropriate timing
jokes.explanation: Good implied visual gag and leadership moment for Gore

jokes.text: Here, take my plus-one mace!
jokes.setup: Fry needs weapon to fight robots
jokes.punchline: D&D creator offers game weapon with stats
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gygax moment - treating real combat like his game, very character-specific

jokes.text: Eternity with nerds. It's a Star Trek convention all over again!
jokes.setup: Characters trapped together after universe destruction
jokes.punchline: Uhura compares to her real-life convention experience
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-joke about the actress's real convention experiences. Self-aware and funny.

jokes.text: I'm a 10th-level vice president.
jokes.setup: Playing D&D after universe destruction
jokes.punchline: Gore's character class is his actual job
jokes.explanation: Clever adaptation of D&D character creation to real world, perfectly fits Gore's character

jokes.text: That was preposterous. Stephen Hawking in a pizzeria.
jokes.setup: Professor dismisses the What-If scenario as unrealistic
jokes.punchline: Of all the absurd things, he fixates on Hawking's location
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about suspension of disbelief - ignores universe destruction to focus on mundane detail

jokes.text: So that's how it would be if I'd invented the Fing-Longer. A man can dream though. A man can dream.
jokes.setup: Episode ends with Professor reflecting
jokes.punchline: Wistful callback to opening invention, breaking fourth wall
jokes.explanation: Perfect bookend callback with philosophical reflection, very quotable and melancholy

tmdb_overview: The Professor's "What-If" machine simulates Bender, Leela and Fry's wishes. Bender discovers what it would be like if he were 500 feet tall, Leela discovers what it would be like if she were more impulsive, and Fry discovers what would happen if he never came to the future.
